symbols: angel with lily branch — Gabriel bearing the symbol of Mary's purity
Mary's startled posture — the humble virgin troubled by the angelic greeting
architectural archway between them — the threshold between heaven and earth
white garment of Mary — her purity and readiness for the divine mission
elevated position of angel — the heavenly messenger descending to earth
wings of the angel — the supernatural origin of the message
description: A tall, narrow composition showing two figures in an elaborate architectural setting. At the upper left, the angel Gabriel hovers above, clothed in flowing robes with large wings, holding a branch of lilies. Below and to the right, the Virgin Mary stands in a white gown beneath a Romanesque archway, her hands clasped at her chest in surprise or acceptance. The architecture features richly carved reliefs and warm golden tones. The figures are elongated and graceful in the Pre-Raphaelite manner.
theological_description: The Annunciation is the pivotal moment of salvation history: the angel Gabriel appears to Mary in Nazareth and announces that she will conceive and bear a son who will be called the Son of the Most High (LUK.1.26-35). Mary's response — 'Let it be to me according to your word' (LUK.1.38) — is the fiat that makes the Incarnation possible. The Church Fathers understood this moment as the reversal of Eve's disobedience: as Eve's 'yes' to the serpent brought the Fall, Mary's 'yes' to the angel inaugurated redemption. The lily traditionally symbolizes Mary's virginity and purity. The architectural setting separating Gabriel above from Mary below represents the gulf between heaven and earth that the Incarnation bridges. Gabriel's message echoes and fulfills Isaiah's prophecy: 'The virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel' (ISA.7.14). This Pre-Raphaelite treatment emphasizes the otherworldly beauty and spiritual gravity of the encounter.

scripture_refs.reason: The Annunciation narrative: Gabriel's message and Mary's fiat
The virgin shall conceive and bear a son — the prophecy fulfilled at the Annunciation
The Holy Spirit will come upon you, the power of the Most High will overshadow you
The seed of the woman — Mary as the new Eve whose offspring will crush the serpent
Emmanuel, God with us — Matthew's citation of Isaiah fulfilled in the Annunciation
